The writing of the Scriptures was by Divine appointment. Divine wisdom directed this as a proper means; if it failed, the people of Judah would be even more without excuse. The Lord declares to sinners the evil he purposes to do against them, that they may hear, fear, and turn from their evil ways; and whenever anyone makes this use of God's warnings, depending on his promised mercy, he will find the Lord ready to forgive his sins. All others will be left without excuse; and considering how great is the anger God has pronounced against us for sin, it should quicken both our prayers and our efforts.

Appearances of piety and devotion may be found even among those who, though they keep up forms of godliness, are strangers and enemies to its power. The princes patiently attended the reading of the whole book. They were in great fear. But even those who are convinced of the truth and importance of what they hear, and are disposed to favor those who preach it, often have difficulties and reservations about their safety, interest, or advancement, so that they do not act according to their convictions, and try to get rid of what they find troublesome.

Those who despise the word of God will soon show, as this king did, that they hate it; and, like him, they would wish it destroyed. See what enmity there is against God in the carnal mind, and wonder at his patience. The princes showed some concern until they saw how lightly the king made of it. Beware of making light of God's word!
